checks.notReachedEnd: You didn't reach the end of the maze.
checks.tooManyLines: Your solution has too many lines of code. Look for groups of consecutive move() calls and replace each group with a repeat loop.
checks.oneLineOver: You're just one line too long! A repeat loop isn't always shorter: repeating something only two or three times can take more lines than writing the calls out, so look for a repeat that isn't actually saving you anything.

solveMazeWithRepeat.name: Refactor the maze solution using repeat loops
solveMazeWithRepeat.description: Replace consecutive `move()` calls with repeat loops to make the code shorter.

mazeRepeat1.name: Refactor the maze solution using repeat loops
mazeRepeat1.description: Use repeat loops to avoid consecutive `move()` calls.

noIdea.question: I have no idea what to do
noIdea.answer:
Look for groups of consecutive `move()` calls. For example, instead of:

```javascript
move()
move()
move()
```

You can write:

```javascript
repeat(3) {
  move()
}
```

Find the groups and replace them!

tooManyLines.question: Using repeats but got too many lines?
tooManyLines.answer: Is it always better to use a repeat? In some cases, it might be shorter to just have two moves next to each other.

move.description: Moves the character **one step forward** in the current direction. The character will only move if the target position is not blocked by a wall.

turnLeft.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the left** (counterclockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.

turnRight.description: Turns the character **90 degrees to the right** (clockwise). This changes the direction the character is facing.
